rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order Directing Ccsf Receiver To Vacate Kbic Void Judgment In Case Number: Sc 124919.
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 27, 2023, line 1. Defendant's Motion for an Order Directing CCSF Receiver to Vacate KBIC Void Judge in case SC 124919 is CONTINUED to February 2, 2024 per the order of the presiding judge. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Meeting ID: 160 854 8108; Passcode: 727961; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Meeting ID: 160 854 8108; Passcode: 727961). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 75 HOWARD OWNER, LP VS. KBSIII 201 SPEAR STREET, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Plaintiff'S Notice Of Pendency Of Action; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 27, 2023, line 2. Defendant's Motion to Expunge Lis Pendens is DENIED. A claim affecting title is asserted in this action re: easement. Park 100 Investment Group II, LLC v. Ryan (2009) 180 Cal.App.4th 795, 812. Sanctions granted in the amount of $7,225. Reply did not address why the sanctions should not be awarded or should not be awarded in the amount requested should the motion be denied. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Meeting ID: 160 854 8108; Passcode: 727961; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Meeting ID: 160 854 8108; Passcode: 727961). rulings.Case Title: RUSS BUILDING VENTURE LLC VS. TOLTEC CAPITAL LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT On Issue Of Possession
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 27, 2023, line 4. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment on Issue of Possession is DENIED. Moving party fails to carry its burden. The motion seeks summary judgment as to the answering defendants, Cohen and Gomez. Moving party correctly states the elements of an unlawful detainer cause of action: (1) the tenant is in possession of the premises; (2) that possession is without permission; (3) the tenant is in default for nonpayment of rent; (4) the tenant has been properly served with a written three-day notice; and (5) the default continues after the three-day notice period has elapsed." Kruger v. Reyes (2014) 232 Cal. App. 4th Supp. 10, 16. Moving party, however, fails to provide any evidence to establish such elements as to the answering defendants, i.e. moving party provides evidence that the defaulted defendant was a tenant obligated to pay rent; defaulted on such obligation; was served with a notice; failed to comply with the notice, and remains in possession. No such evidence is provided as to the answering defendants. To the contrary, supporting declaration suggests that Plaintiff has no landlord-tenant relationship with the answering defendants. In passing, moving party appears to suggest that the answers have been improperly filed without a filing of a pre-judgment claim of right to possession per CCP 415.46. (Points and Authorities 6:9-10). No analysis for this statement is provided. To the extent, the moving party suggests that the answers have been improperly filed and should be stricken, such motion cannot be considered as it was not set in accordance with CCP 1005. Therefore, the Court will not make any ruling on the issue of propriety of the answers at this time. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Meeting ID: 160 854 8108; Passcode: 727961; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Meeting ID: 160 854 8108; Passcode: 727961). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/REQ)
